summary_line: Player tracking and field projection stayed mostly intact across 250 frames, but ball coverage was very sparse and team counts were imbalanced.

diagnostics.title: Field mapping held
diagnostics.message: Field registration covered 2708 of 2721 player rows, a 0.9952 ratio. Calibration refresh succeeded 19 times out of 25 attempts, and the last good calibration was at frame 240 of 250.
diagnostics.next_step: Use the projected player coordinates for this clip, but inspect the final 10 frames where calibration was no longer refreshed.

diagnostics.title: Ball tracking is sparse
diagnostics.message: Only 19 ball rows were produced across 250 frames, or 0.076 detections per frame. There is just 1 ball track ID, so continuity exists when detected, but coverage is too thin for possession or event review.
diagnostics.next_step: Re-run this clip with ball detection as the priority and manually verify whether the ball is visible in most frames before using ball-derived outputs.

diagnostics.title: Team counts are skewed
diagnostics.message: Assigned tracks are 23 home and 11 away, with 0 unassigned. Average player detections are 10.884 per frame, which is below a full 22-player view, but the 2.1:1 home-away split still points to missed away detections or team assignment drift.
diagnostics.next_step: Spot-check away-player visibility and jersey separation in several frames, then re-run team clustering if the away side is visibly undercounted.

diagnostics.title: Track stitching reduced fragments
diagnostics.message: Raw unique player track IDs dropped from 34 to 23 after 11 merges, a stitched track ID reduction of 0.323529. Longest track length reached all 250 frames, while average track length was 80.0294 frames.
diagnostics.next_step: Keep stitched tracks enabled for this setup, then review short residual tracks to see whether more merges are safe.

diagnostics.title: Experimental signal is not goal-aligned
diagnostics.message: The only experiment is marked not goal-aligned, and the clip has 0 goal events. Its average volatility index is 0.0, pre-goal index is 0.0, baseline index is 0.0, and uplift is 0.0, so there is no event-linked readout to interpret here.
diagnostics.next_step: Do not use the volatility experiment for decision-making on this run; test it on clips with annotated goals or other target events.
